c74object.name: voice-alligator-looper2
c74object.description:
This Note Looper records note events from voice-alligator and plays them back in a loop on a configurable stream.
It remembers exact pitches, velocities, portamento / glide settings, and timing of a musical sequence. Notes held when recording stops are handled gracefully via overlap detection, and notes that span the loop boundary (wrapping notes) are preserved across cycles.
Recorded loops can be quantized to a rhythmic grid, overdubbed, permutated, transposed, and played back at variable speed. Volume and pitch jitter add humanization.
If the note looper is set to a higher stream than an input going into voice-alligator, it will by default not steal notes from that input.

inlet.description: Receives output from voice-alligator for recording. Also accepts the looper's own messages and attributes.
Auxiliary input for parameter recording (not yet implemented).

outlet.description: Sends recorded note data back to voice-alligator in sequencer format: target, velocity, stream, glide_flag, frequency.
Auxiliary output for recorded parameter data (not yet implemented).

Outputs status notifications:
play 1 / 0 — playback started or stopped
record 1 / 0 — recording started or stopped
overdub 1 / 0 — overdub started or stopped
quantize_done — quantization completed

objarg.name: stream
objarg.description:
Sets which stream the looper will output to. Higher streams have lower priority in voice-alligator's voice allocation.
default 2

method.name: list
method.description: Messages from voice-alligator. These are lists that start with "target" followed by pitch, velocity, "flags", and flag values. The looper parses these to record note-on, note-off, and glide events with their exact timing and pitch.

method.name: record
method.description:
record 1 starts recording. If playback is active, it is stopped first and all sounding notes are killed. Any previously recorded events are cleared.
record 0 stops recording and determines the loop length. If autoplay is enabled, playback starts immediately. Notes still held when recording stops become overlap notes whose note-off is captured when the player releases the key.

method.name: play
method.description:
play 1 starts looped playback of the recorded sequence.
play 0 stops playback and sends note-offs for all potentially sounding voices.

method.name: clear
method.description: Stops playback and clears all recorded events, resetting the looper to its initial state.

method.name: overdub
method.description:
Enables or disables overdub recording on top of an existing loop.
overdub 1 next — arm overdub; recording starts on the next note-on.
overdub 1 now — start overdub immediately (notes currently held are ignored).
overdub 1 next N — arm overdub for N loop cycles, then stop automatically.
overdub 1 now inf — overdub indefinitely until manually stopped.
overdub 0 — stop overdub and merge recorded events into the loop.

method.name: permutate
method.description:
Randomly rearranges the timing of recorded notes while preserving their durations.
permutate 0 — full shuffle of all note positions.
permutate N (N > 0) — perform N random slot swaps.

attribute.name: speed
attribute.description:
Sets the playback speed as a ratio. 1.0 is normal speed, 2.0 is double speed, 0.5 is half speed.
default 1.0

attribute.name: record_pressed
attribute.digest: inject pressed notes as note-ons on record start
attribute.description:
If a note is being held when recording starts, transform it into a note-on at the beginning of the loop. This makes timing a loop easier since you can hold a note before pressing record.
default true

attribute.name: autoplay
attribute.description:
When enabled, playback starts automatically as soon as recording stops (provided notes were recorded).
default true

attribute.name: autostop_play
attribute.description:
When enabled, playback is stopped when recording is turned on. When disabled, playback is always stopped before recording regardless (to prevent timer conflicts), but this attribute controls whether the stop notification is sent.
default false

attribute.name: vol
attribute.description:
Multiplies recorded velocities by the given percentage.
default 100.0

attribute.name: volJitter
attribute.description:
Randomly subtracts up to N% from each note's velocity on playback, adding humanization.
default 0

attribute.name: transpose
attribute.description:
Transposes all recorded notes by the given number of semitones. Fractional values are supported for microtonal transposition.
default 0.0

attribute.name: pitchJitter
attribute.description:
Randomly offsets each note's pitch by up to +/- N semitones on playback.
default 0.0

attribute.name: quantize
attribute.description:
Sets the quantization grid and triggers quantization when set during runtime. Accepted values are standard note divisions: 1n, 2n, 4n, 8n, 16n, 32n, and their dotted and triplet variants (e.g. 8nd, 8nt). Only note-on start times are quantized; durations are preserved.
default 8n

attribute.name: quantize_mode
attribute.description:
Controls when quantization takes effect:
next — apply immediately, skipping events that have already elapsed in the current loop cycle.
wrap — apply at the next loop wrap point for a seamless transition.
default next

attribute.name: autoquantize
attribute.digest: auto-quantize on record stop and overdub wrap
attribute.description:
When enabled, automatically quantizes the loop when recording stops or when an overdub cycle wraps.
default false

attribute.name: autotempo
attribute.description:
When enabled, the BPM for quantization is detected automatically from Max's global transport. When disabled, the value of the bpm attribute is used instead.
default true

attribute.name: bpm
attribute.description:
Sets the BPM used for quantization when autotempo is off.
default 120.0

attribute.name: debug
attribute.description:
Prints detailed event-level debug info to the Max console: note-on/off events during recording and playback, loop wrap decisions, quantization results, and playback start/stop notifications.
default false
